matches.moment: Diallo's direct run splits the Curaçao right side in the 38th; Adingra punishes a tired defensive shape late — Room was extraordinary vs. Ecuador but one extraordinary performance is variance, not baseline.

Germany scored 3+ in 8 of their last 9 internationals; Ecuador have 0 goals across 2 games with their creator suspended — Undav opens early, ECU chase the game and collapse defensively, Beier and Goretzka punish the space.

Sweden's must-win desperation forces them to push 5 men forward from the 60th minute — Japan's 3-4-3 transitions become lethal; Doan's late counter kills the game as Sweden's defensive line is stranded.

Tunisia conceded 5 to Sweden and 4 to Japan — Japan scored 4 here at this same tournament. Netherlands' GD race with Japan means Koeman keeps pushing long after the result is settled; Summerville adds the 4th in the 82nd.

USA scored 4 and 2 in their first two games — even rotating they finish. Güler finally gets his tournament goal but the USA's superior finishing efficiency (6 goals on 2.47 xG vs Turkey's 0 on ~2.5 xG) decides it.

A Paraguay corner from the right — Romero's flick creates chaos in the six-yard box; Gómez pounces on the second ball after Souttar's clearance falls short; Australia's shape collapses for the only time in the match.

verdict: This tournament is averaging 3.1 goals per game — the highest group-stage rate in decades — and today's six-match final slate will not be an exception. The pattern is brutal and consistent: Germany have scored 3+ in eight of their last nine internationals, and against an Ecuador side that has produced zero goals across two matches and now plays without its primary creator in Páez, a 3-0 is the minimum honest projection, not a ceiling. The Netherlands face a Tunisia defence that has leaked nine goals in two games, with a goal-difference race against Japan providing the exact motivation needed to keep pushing long after the result is settled — 4-0 is where the data points. Japan's counter-attacking system, already devastating against a weaker Tunisia, becomes a scoring machine when Sweden must commit forwards in the 60th minute to stay alive. The USA, for all the rotation caution, have still outscored their xG at a remarkable rate across this group stage and finish this group as they started it — by scoring more than the opposition. The single genuinely tight match is Paraguay versus Australia, where Almirón's suspension and Australia's structural incentive to park the bus create the one legitimate case for a 1-0 result. Everywhere else today, the goals are coming. — The Analyst.

players.name: Ayase Ueda
players.watch: Exploit Sweden's high defensive line on Japan's third-man runs — scores in the 18th before Sweden can even establish their shape

players.name: Arda Güler
players.stat: 0 tournament goals, est. 1.3 xG/game created — most creative on an eliminated side
players.watch: The tournament goal that was coming — curling finish from 20m after Çalhanoğlu diagonal; arrives too late to change the result

players.name: Cody Gakpo
players.stat: 1 tournament goal; NED averaging 1.70 xG/game; market over/under vs. TUN set at 3.5
players.watch: Opens the scoring in the 12th — Netherlands start fast to bank GD before Japan can set a high target in the parallel game

players.name: Deniz Undav
players.stat: 5 sub goal contributions this tournament (ties Roger Milla's all-time WC bench record); starts today
players.watch: First full 90 vs. Ecuador — scores early to end any lingering ECU hope; frees Germany to control the rest

players.name: Viktor Gyökeres
players.stat: 7 qualifying goals (fallback — 0 WC tournament goals yet)
players.watch: Scores through Isak to make it 1-1 at 38' but Sweden's defensive exposure on the counter undoes the fight

players.name: Simon Adingra
players.stat: Regular PL starter for Brighton 2025/26 — pace and direct running on the left
players.watch: Punishes Curaçao's tired legs in the 74th minute — Room made 15 saves vs. Ecuador; this time the volume breaks through
